policy.note: Declared blocker status is policy, not observed run state. The closure runner derives observed resolution from exact receipts or an approved unexpired waiver bound to the effective candidate digest (metadata plus payload content address), never metadata alone; foundational source binding is non-waivable. A local pass is not a release pass.

requiredEvidence: identical source commit SHA, clean tree and canonical repository identity before and after closure
stable source inventory and fingerprint matched by a content-verified Ralph candidate receipt or manifest
candidate repository, commit and fingerprint bound to the same source without archiving the raw remote URL
content-addressed closure envelope whose manifest and checksums cover the source-binding receipt

waiver.rationale: Source identity is foundational evidence and no candidate-bound waiver was supplied.

requiredEvidence: opt-in real API-key or account-auth smoke with redacted output
ChatGPT Plus or Pro device/browser authorization smoke where supported
quota, revocation and keychain behavior bound to the candidate

waiver.rationale: Mocks and protocol fixtures cannot establish access to a real account or provider.

requiredEvidence: docs/reviews/r015-parser-static-review.json with independent review identity and APPROVED verdict
review claims proving regex is limited to leaf tokens rather than raw Markdown
current packages/prd/src/parser.ts bytes, line count and SHA-256 exactly matching the receipt

waiver.rationale: No waiver is granted. The declared blocker is observed as resolved only while the independent receipt remains exactly bound to the current parser.

requiredEvidence: remote forge fixture or controlled remote proving PR creation and integration
explicit product decision for unsupported remote operations if any
candidate-bound branch, commit and PR receipts

waiver.rationale: Local Git fixtures do not prove behavior against a remote forge.

requiredEvidence: Docker and Podman lifecycle evidence on supported hosts
strong-isolation network, mount, process and cleanup receipts
candidate-bound safe, auto and dangerous policy matrix

waiver.rationale: Process-only local tests cannot represent container or strong sandbox guarantees.

requiredEvidence: successful blocking CI runs for Windows, Linux and macOS on x64 and arm64
JUnit classification and content-addressed artifacts for all six target pairs
approved explicit waiver for any unavailable runner, never a silent skip

waiver.rationale: Only the local Windows x64 target has observed runtime evidence.

requiredEvidence: approved product identity, license, support policy, channels and target list
candidate SBOM, third-party notices, provenance, signatures and trust policy
install, update, downgrade, rollback and uninstall drills on real candidate packages
promotion record bound to immutable candidate digests

waiver.rationale: No release owner decisions, signed candidate or promotion record exists.

requiredEvidence: explicit --legacy-binary and --next-binary regular non-symlink inputs
binary SHA-256 values matching the archived S10 compatibility report
91/91 compatibility result with zero regressions bound to those exact binaries

waiver.rationale: The closure runner must not infer or download a Ralph v1 binary.
